mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically increasing present valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0 percent, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending expectations and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 6.0, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production rises while housing starts fall indicates supply chain bottlenecks and input cost inflation, which compresses equity margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO up, HOUST down) fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of observed instances, or the joint signal shows no incremental explanatory power over INDPRO alone.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a rising federal funds rate signals an active monetary tightening cycle that increases the equity risk premium and discount rates, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of periods where both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS trend upward, or the combined signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in real retail and food services sales directly reflects strengthening aggregate demand, which translates into higher corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS growth fails to precede positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 60% of observations, or the rolling Spearman correlation between RSAFS changes and SPX 5-day returns remains below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP growth signals a demand-side recession that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate falls below 1.5% annualized
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in more than 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth drops below 1.5%, or the signal exhibits no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS increase and UNRATE decline fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 60% of instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS trajectory.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in housing starts acts as a leading indicator for broader construction activity and consumer wealth effects, which boosts equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months where HOUST jumps above the 5% threshold, or the signal's predictive power disappears after adjusting for seasonal housing cycles.
